target.title: Reply-context body truncation: cover ReplyChain and fallback ReplyToBody JSON paths
target.body:
Follow-up to #87291 / #87909.

#87909 introduces `sanitizeTranscriptBody` (head + tail truncation with explicit omission marker) and applies it to two reply-context paths:

- `formatChatWindowMessage` body (chat_window entries)
- `resolveInlineReplyQuote` (Telegram inline `[Replying to: "..."]`)

Two other reply-context paths in the same file still go through the generic JSON-block sanitizer and get **prefix-only truncation at `MAX_UNTRUSTED_JSON_STRING_CHARS = 2000`** (`src/auto-reply/reply/inbound-meta.ts:14`):

1. **`buildReplyChainPayload`** (l.303–) — `entry.body` enters the JSON block via `sanitizePromptBody` (l.308), then every string in the JSON payload is truncated by `sanitizeUntrustedJsonValue` → `truncateUntrustedJsonString` (l.64–70). This is the path used for the deep reply chain from Gateway's SQLite `telegram/message-cache`.
2. **Fallback `ReplyToBody` JSON block** (l.583, l.593) — when no chat_window / Telegram-inline context covers reply state, `replyToBody` is rendered as its own `formatUntrustedJsonBlock("Reply target of current user message ...", { body })`, again subject to the same 2000-char prefix-only cap.

That's the *opposite* of what one might expect: the inline shallow quote (Telegram-supplied, single-update, no Gateway-provenance) now gets a generous 6000-char head+tail, while the more structured / Gateway-cached deep chain gets a prefix-only 2000.

## Why the natural fix doesn't compose

Calling `sanitizeTranscriptBody(entry.body)` upstream in `buildReplyChainPayload` doesn't help: `sanitizeUntrustedJsonValue` recursively re-truncates every string at 2000 chars downstream, so the head+tail content just gets re-clipped to a prefix anyway.

## Proposed shape

Two non-exclusive approaches:

**(a)** In-place head+tail truncation at the JSON entry point, tighter than chat_window because the chain is multi-message and attacker-controlled:

- nearest reply target body (chain[0]): head + tail ≤ 2000 (so the downstream JSON sanitizer is a pass-through)
- next ancestor: ≤ 1500
- older ancestors: ≤ 800
- aggregate chain block: hard cap (e.g. 6000)

**(b)** Render the reply chain as line-based (like chat_window) instead of a JSON block, which decouples it from `MAX_UNTRUSTED_JSON_STRING_CHARS` entirely and lets it share the same body sanitizer as chat_window.

Tiering / numbers above are policy sketch — would defer to maintainer judgment.

## Suggested tests

- `buildReplyChainPayload_usesHeadTailForNearestReplyBody` — nearest entry preserves actionable tail.
- `buildReplyChainPayload_appliesSmallerBudgetToAncestors` — chain depth N can't each consume the full nearest budget.
- `sanitizeTranscriptBody_preservesTailForFallbackReplyToBodyJsonBlock` — fallback path no longer prefix-only.

Linking #87291 for context, #87909 for the in-progress fix on the chat_window + inline-quote paths.
target.author: wangwllu
